summary: Frontend regression pass for the 'Luxury Dark' theme pivot of SaniClock. All 7 acceptance criteria PASS end-to-end. (1) /login renders LUXURY DARK: body bg rgb(8,9,12) obsidian, body text rgb(243,245,249); left pane shows 'SaniClock / POWERED BY SANIXPERTS' wordmark, bold 'Welcome back.' heading, USERNAME/PASSWORD inputs, gradient blue 'Sign in' button, and '256-bit encrypted session · SaniXperts' cue; right pane shows a large live clock ticking (05:16:38 → 05:16:40), 'Friday, Jul 17, 2026' date, feature chips ('24 / 7 Coverage', '3 shifts Rotating', 'Auto Payroll'), a large 'Attendance rhythm' SVG activity graph (1x .gline, 1x .garea, 1x .gdot, plus grid axis + LIVE SYNC pill), and three shift cards (Day/Afternoon/Night) with the active Night card highlighted with '.now' class + 'ON NOW' pill + '6h 16m elapsed · 1h 44m left' live progress text. All required data-testids present (login-form, login-username-input, login-password-input, login-submit-btn, live-clock). (2) admin/Saniclock2026! posts to /api/login and redirects to '/'; dashboard renders DARK (body bg rgb(8,9,12)) with blue active sidebar item (Absence Requests highlighted in electric-blue). (3) #navAbsence is a full nav item with pending badge '2'; clicking opens #absenceView (not hidden); #absSeg reads 'Pending 2 | Approved 0 | Rejected 0 | All 2'; #absRows lists both seeded requests (Aisha Khan/1001/Vacation/Family trip and Marco Rossi/1002/Sick/Flu); clicking [data-aact='approve'] on first pending row updates segbar to 'Pending 1 | Approved 1 | Rejected 0 | All 2'. (4) /me renders DARK (body text rgb(243,245,249)); force-showing #portalView reveals #openAbs button; clicking it opens #absOv sheet (class='ov on') with #aType (SELECT, default Vacation), #aStart & #aEnd (input type=date, default 07/17/2026), #aReason (input), #aSubmit ('Submit for approval'). (5) After admin login, /m loads DARK (bg rgb(8,9,12), text rgb(243,245,249)); KPI cards visible (On roster, Present, On floor, Absent — all 0 due to no seed data + 'No records' empty state); bottom nav .nav has 4 buttons — 3 with data-pg (Today/People/Devices) plus 'Full' (onclick=location.href='/'); clicking [data-pg='People'] then [data-pg='Devices'] applies '.on' class to nav button and visually transitions to that page ('Devices' → 'No devices yet' empty state screenshot confirmed). (6) Contrast/readability — no unreadable elements on any of the 4 pages; only heuristic false positive was HTML root element containing inline <style> text (not user-visible). (7) No new JS console errors on /login, /, /m; the only errors observed are the 2 expected 401s on /api/my-punches from /me unauthenticated polling.

design_issues.screen: /login shift cards (carryover a11y polish, not a new regression)
issues: The 'ON NOW' markup is still emitted inside all three shift cards; only the '.now' (active) card visually shows it because CSS hides '.sfbadge' on non-active cards. Screenreaders may announce 'ON NOW' three times. Optional a11y polish: emit .sfbadge only when cur===key.

action_items: OPTIONAL a11y polish (carried from iter-2/3, still applies): in LOGIN_HTML shift-card template, only emit the .sfbadge 'ON NOW' span when the shift is the active one (cur === key), rather than emitting it on all three and hiding two via CSS.

OPTIONAL console cleanup: /me polls /api/my-punches on page load without a session and surfaces the 401 as a browser console error. Add a lightweight session probe (or catch/swallow the 401) so /me console is clean for genuine debugging.
critical_code_review_comments: scale.js remains a single ~3600+ line monolith. Strongly recommend splitting LOGIN_HTML, ME_HTML, MOBILE_HTML, page() dashboard, and API handlers into separate modules (e.g., views/login.js, views/me.js, views/dashboard.js, views/mobile.js, api/absence.js). Each theme pivot re-touches every string in this file.

The mobile PWA (/m) uses 'Full' as a 4th bottom-nav button with a raw onclick=location.href='/' handler instead of the .nav [data-pg] pattern used by the other three buttons. Consider unifying — either give it data-pg and handle inside the JS router, or move it out of the same visual row so users don't expect the same transition behavior.

The heuristic contrast scan flagged the HTML root element because getComputedStyle picks up inline <style> textContent inside the tree walk — a benign false positive for us, but the same class of bug would silently include <script> and <style> textContent in any real 'find broken text' audits. If any future automated a11y scanner is added, filter by :visible + non-<script>/<style>.

LOGIN_HTML activity graph is currently a static/decorative SVG (hard-coded path). The 'LIVE SYNC' pill implies real-time data. If it's intentionally decorative, remove the LIVE SYNC pill or de-emphasize it to avoid misleading users; otherwise wire it to a real endpoint.
updated_files: /app/data/absence-requests.json (restored to 2 pending before test to match acceptance criteria; approving Aisha again as part of test — post-test state: 1 pending Marco Rossi + 1 approved Aisha Khan)
test_credentials: Admin admin / Saniclock2026! at /login — verified working. No employee accounts seeded; /me portal tested by force-showing #portalView per instructions.
seed_data_creation: Restored /app/data/absence-requests.json to 2 pending (Aisha Khan Vacation + Marco Rossi Sick) before testing since iter-3 left one approved. Test then approved Aisha so final state = 1 approved (Aisha) + 1 pending (Marco). If next iteration needs a clean 2-pending state, restore the file again.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Luxury Dark redesign fully working end-to-end and functionally identical to iterations 2 and 3. Post-run absence-requests.json state = 1 approved (Aisha Khan/Vacation) + 1 pending (Marco Rossi/Sick); restore to 2 pending if the next iteration needs a clean state. Note: on /m the 'Full' button uses onclick=location.href='/' not data-pg, so any playwright test asserting all four bottom-nav buttons carry data-pg will fail — the acceptance criterion only asked for People and Devices transitions, which do work. meta theme-color on /me and /m is now #05070e which correctly matches the dark obsidian bg (was flagged in iter-3 under the light theme, now aligned). The only remaining optional polish items are the same two carried from iter-2/iter-3: conditional-render 'ON NOW' badge on /login, and swallow the /me 401 console noise.
rca of the issue: n/a — no regressions detected. All acceptance criteria met.
